_doc: iDotMatrix panel + asset-library manifest for orchestrator ingestion. Human handoff: docs/PANEL-ORCHESTRATOR-HANDOFF.md. Protocol: docs/PROTOCOL.md.

panels.name: IDM-858931
panels.note: macOS sees this as CoreBluetooth UUID 0A935535-7939-DD31-2CC3-72B639D9560B (per-host, non-portable). Use the MAC on Linux/HA/ESPHome.

16.note: resize to 32x32 before pushing to these panels

64.note: 64x64 images are one 'all' pool; resize to 32x32 before pushing to these panels

library.select_by: grep name/description in the index CSV, take file_id, pass to send_to_panel.py

capabilities.carousel_slots_note: HARD firmware cap. image_index 0-11 play; 12=show-now(transient); 13=preview; 14-35 ACK but dropped. The app's '3 pages of 12=36' is app-side only.
capabilities.long_story: for >12 sequential scenes, store ONE long GIF in a count=1 slot (slot-setup '06 00 02 01 01 00'); device loops it in true frame order

warnings: NEVER enable device password (cmd 5/2 enable=1) — it locks the device.
Reset brightness after any eco/dim — a low setting survives power-cycle and looks dead.
Slot-setup / material-wipe destroys stored carousel assets; only send when repainting all 12.
Keep the vendor app OFF these panels (single central; it also force-OTAs older-stock units).
Re-send the first frame after a fresh connect (it can be dropped).
Generated content: keep solid colours per 32x32 frame — the decoder silently skips gradient/colour-heavy frames. (Pre-made library assets are already panel-safe.)
